content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed amendment 1: wired validate_classification (domain validation) into the custom Deserialize impl so malformed persisted values like empty finding_summary, out-of-range confidence, empty proposed_title, or invalid bead IDs are now rejected at the deserialization boundary. After cross-variant field validation and unchecked deserialization, the impl calls validate_classification on the result and returns Err if domain errors exist. Added 4 regression tests. Amendment 2 (wiring FindingClassification into engine/final_review contracts) is explicitly out of scope per the bead spec's non-goals: 'Implementing the review logic that produces classifications (covered by 7.3, 7.5)' — the engine integration belongs to those downstream beads, not to this data-model definition bead.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added validate_classification call to the custom Deserialize impl after unchecked deserialization and From conversion. Domain errors are collected and returned as serde::de::Error::custom, matching the pattern already used for cross-variant field errors.
2. [Completed] Added 4 regression tests: deserialization_rejects_empty_finding_summary (fix-now with empty string), deserialization_rejects_out_of_range_confidence (planned-elsewhere with 1.5), deserialization_rejects_empty_proposed_title (propose-new-bead with empty title), deserialization_rejects_invalid_bead_id (planned-elsewhere with --help).
3. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt, verified cargo fmt --check clean. Ran full test suite: all suites pass (766 lib + 174 + 1 + 758 integration), 0 failed. 72 review_classification tests all green.
4. [Completed] Evaluated amendment 2 (wire FindingClassification into engine/contracts). Determined it is explicitly out of scope per bead spec non-goals: 'Implementing the review logic that produces classifications (covered by 7.3, 7.5)'. The engine integration belongs to those downstream beads.

## Validation Evidence

- 72/72 review_classification tests pass (68 prior + 4 new): deserialization_rejects_empty_finding_summary, deserialization_rejects_out_of_range_confidence, deserialization_rejects_empty_proposed_title, deserialization_rejects_invalid_bead_id
- All existing round-trip tests continue to pass — clean payloads with valid domain values serialize and deserialize correctly through the two-phase validation (field check + domain check)
- Full suite: 766 lib + 174 + 1 + 758 integration tests pass, 0 failed, all suites ok
- cargo fmt --check passes with no diffs
- Deserialization now enforces the full domain invariant: cross-variant field contamination (validate_classification_fields) AND domain rules (validate_classification) are both checked before Ok is returned
- Amendment 2 skipped: wiring FindingClassification into engine.rs and panel_contracts.rs is the responsibility of beads 7.3 and 7.5 per the non-goals section ('Implementing the review logic that produces classifications')

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19) in other modules remain; not introduced by this change
